title: Pine and juniper solid perfume
subtitle: A pocket-sized balm scented with Scots pine and juniper berry
excerpt: A beeswax and jojoba solid perfume scented with Scots pine and juniper berry essential oils. The balm format is travel-friendly and applies without the risk of spilling. The fragrance is green, resinous, and slightly astringent.
sourceNotes: Solid perfume formulation: beeswax and liquid carrier oil at roughly 30:70 ratio for a firm balm that applies cleanly without feeling greasy. Essential oil usage rate for perfume balms (leave-on, skin contact): Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Pine and juniper berry at 2.5% combined are within safe limits for a leave-on product applied to pulse points.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep essential oil content at 2 to 3% of formula weight for a safe leave-on perfume.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 1 hour to set fully before capping the tin.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the tin away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. The pine top note fades before the juniper base. Discard if the oil smells rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. This solid perfume keeps 12 months without a preservative.

The temperature at which beeswax fully liquefies. In a solid perfume, the beeswax sets the balm firm enough to apply by rubbing against skin but soft enough that a small amount transfers easily at skin temperature.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours before applying to pulse points. Pine and juniper berry essential oils are generally well-tolerated on skin at these concentrations.

content.text: Solid perfume is a balm carrier (beeswax and a liquid oil) with a higher essential oil load than a typical skin balm. The format is convenient: no liquid, no atomiser, pocket-sized. The fragrance transfers to skin by rubbing the tin against a pulse point. Scots pine and juniper berry together are a green, resinous combination: pine brings a fresh, slightly camphorous note; juniper adds a sharper, gin-like quality. Both are base-to-mid notes that hold reasonably well through body heat. At 2.5% combined essential oil, this is within the standard leave-on skin-safe range. Because this is an
product, no preservative is needed. The
of the beeswax (62 to 65°C) keeps the balm firm at room temperature but the 30% beeswax to 70% jojoba ratio means the surface softens readily with gentle contact.

items.prepNote: 30% of formula; sets the balm to a firm, portable consistency

content.text: Weigh the beeswax and jojoba oil into the top of a small double boiler. Melt over gently simmering water until the wax is fully liquid. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 55 to 60°C. Add both essential oils and stir to combine. Pour into the small metal tin immediately before the wax begins to set. Allow 1 hour to cool completely before capping.
on the inside of the forearm for 24 to 48 hours before applying to pulse points.

Rub a fingertip gently against the surface of the tin to pick up a small amount of balm. Apply to pulse points: wrists, neck, or inside the elbows. The body heat at pulse points warms the balm and releases the fragrance. A small amount is enough; build up if needed.
Store in the tin away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. The pine top note fades before the juniper base. Discard if the jojoba oil develops a rancid smell.
